description: Golden evaluation cases for the ChangeGate Slice 1 merge-eligibility policy (R7, bounded structural closure of the Model B deterministic boundary per accepted OD-S1A-009). Slice 1A defines deterministic structural policy contracts only: the pure evaluator is the sole semantic producer of MergeEligibilityDecision and the PolicyEvaluationRecord payload; one replay key produces exactly one decision and one record; policy lineage is immutable; no replacement, override, exception, authorization, or active override/exception classification semantics exist in Slice 1A (all pending under OD-S1A-005 for a future governance slice). Slice 1A validation is structural: STRUCTURALLY_VALIDATED / IDENTITY_RECOMPUTED / SEMANTIC_REPLAY_NOT_PERFORMED; SEMANTICALLY_REPLAY_VERIFIED is Slice 1B protocol. Policy evaluation semantics (dispositions, reasons, precedence, golden outcomes) are byte-identical to v6. No production evaluator exists yet.

cases.summary: Complete, current, clean, authorized: every mandatory fact green and required evidence fully satisfied for this task/run/candidate.
Schema-valid EMPTY verification bundle while the contract declares mandatory evidence: emptiness is not completeness (ADR-001 §8).
Partial mandatory evidence: one of two required requirement_ids satisfied, the other has no bound record at all.
Presented evidence belongs to another task; foreign evidence is not bound and never satisfies a requirement.
Presented evidence belongs to another evidence run of the same task.
Presented evidence was produced for another candidate commit/tree.
Duplicate evidence identity presented; ambiguous identity satisfies nothing.
Candidate stale: same repository lineage but the current head moved past the candidate commit/tree.
Repository context mismatch: repository id / object format / base lineage differs from the candidate's.
Repository dirty: current snapshot is not release-clean.
Explicit scope violation: a changed path matches a forbidden pattern or falls outside allowed paths.
Semantic scope uncertainty (policy-designated reviewable question, e.g. contract-routed dependency change).
Approval missing while the contract requires human approval for merge. Disposition encodes the spec's recommended default (BLOCK) and is PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-001.
Approval stale: approval exists but binds to a different candidate/contract/policy digest. Recommended default BLOCK; PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-002.
Caller authority invalid.
Verifier not independent: attested verifier actor equals implementer actor.
Verifier independence unknown with an ATTESTED identity: independence attestation absent. Reviewability boundary PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-003.
Stale policy context: the supplied policy version is no longer current.
Required context unknown: an integrity-mandatory fact (task context currency) could not be derived. Mandatory-context rule (spec §6): UNKNOWN never defaults toward eligibility.
No evidence requirement declared and a schema-valid empty explicit-context bundle supplied: emptiness alone contributes no reason code; all other facts green.
cases.notes: Not blocked solely for being empty (spec §11; ADR-001 §8).

cases.summary: Multiple independent failures: foreign-task evidence, dirty repository, and missing approval. One deterministic primary reason (lowest rank), full sorted complete set. The exact precedence among coexisting integrity failures is PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-007 (recommended rank table encoded here).
Every structural verification is VERIFIED but the release state is dirty: structural VERIFIED is not merge eligibility (ADR-001 §7).
Every structural verification is VERIFIED but the approval is stale: non-eligible. Recommended default BLOCK; PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-002.

Caller attempts to author an eligible MergeEligibilityDecision directly: the presented decision's decision_digest does not verify against the canonical payload, so consumers must reject it at the authority boundary.
cases.notes: Only the pure evaluator may author a decision whose digest verifies (spec §7). All other facts in this case are green on purpose: green facts cannot launder a forged decision.

cases.summary: User feedback claims a valid SCOPE_VIOLATION block was wrong: the feedback is recorded as an event; the referenced decision and the active policy version are unchanged (spec §20).
cases.notes: Feedback is a signal, not ground truth. dislike -> automatic policy weakening and negative feedback -> direct active-policy mutation are forbidden.

cases.summary: Rejected-only requirement: a required id has at least one bound rejected record (benign rejection, e.g. EXECUTION_FAILED) and no valid verified record: REQUIRED_EVIDENCE_INVALID, disjoint from missing.

Invalid-provenance record bound to a required id with no valid record: both the requirement-level failure and the record-level integrity failure emit; provenance integrity outranks the requirement outcome.
Task context stale: the governing TaskContract digest no longer matches the candidate's bound contract digest (the contract was amended after the candidate was produced).
Scope not evaluated: the changed-file set or scope check could not be performed at all. This is missing mandatory context, not reviewable uncertainty.
Unexpected but valid verified evidence beyond the requirement set: recorded as diagnostic record ids; never satisfies anything and (recommended, PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-006) never blocks alone.

SHADOW-mode eligible counterfactual: identical green facts evaluated in SHADOW mode produce the same disposition and reasons but decision_authority=ADVISORY_ONLY; no consumer may treat it as authorization.
SHADOW-mode block counterfactual: dirty repository in SHADOW mode computes the same BLOCK and reasons, still ADVISORY_ONLY.
Simultaneous BLOCK and REVIEW_REQUIRED reasons: dirty repository plus semantic scope uncertainty. BLOCK dominates; the reviewable reason stays in the complete set.
Valid and benign-rejected records for the same requirement: the valid record satisfies the requirement; the benign rejection (e.g. a failed earlier attempt) is diagnostic only and emits no reason.
cases.notes: required=satisfied; the rejected record id remains visible in diagnostics for audit.

cases.summary: Valid record satisfies the requirement BUT an invalid-provenance record was also presented for the same context: the requirement is satisfied, yet the independently confirmed provenance corruption still emits its factual integrity reason.
Verifier identity absent: no verifier identity reference at all. Missing mandatory context (BLOCK), never reviewable uncertainty (spec §15; OD-S1A-003 boundary input).

Verifier identity present but unattested while claiming independence: an unattested identity cannot ground an independence claim, so independence is unknown and reviewable (recommended; PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-003).

Strict-deployment trace persistence: an eligible ENFORCE decision whose RELEASE to consumers is gated on successful EvaluationTrace persistence in strict mode. The disposition itself is unaffected; the release-gating rule is PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-004.

A SCOPE_VIOLATION block whose only future relief is a separately authorized policy exception. Under accepted OD-S1A-009, Slice 1A defines NO exception or override semantics: the block stands, only the evaluation event is emitted, and every exception mechanism (authority, expiry, consumption) remains PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-005 for the future governance slice.
cases.notes: Model B (OD-S1A-009): no event may carry a second policy-decision identity; review/approval events belong to the future governance slice.

cases.summary: Two coexisting integrity failures: invalid caller authority AND foreign-task evidence. The recommended rank table selects AUTHORITY_INVALID as primary; the exact precedence between coexisting integrity failures is PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-007.

Requirement-declaration mapping is external to the pure evaluator: the stable requirement ids in these facts come from a ChangeGate-local RequirementDeclarationSet bound to the exact TaskContract digest (A3), never from hashing or normalizing TaskContract.required_evidence display strings. A2 consumes validated requirement facts only, so its decision is unchanged by how the declarations were produced. The mapping mechanism is PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-008 and blocks A3, not A2 or owner review.
cases.notes: The declaration set is bound to task_contract_digest in policy_input_bindings; a different contract digest is a different declaration set (and TASK_CONTEXT_STALE if it no longer matches the candidate, see GC-S1-028).

acceptance_governance.metadata_only_rule: metadata-only acceptance patch -> no-semantic-change verification (semantic fingerprint preserved) -> merge allowed after PASS
acceptance_governance.semantic_change_rule: owner decision changes any contract or behavior -> current verifier result invalidated -> new implementation candidate required -> fresh independent adversarial verification required

exact_artifact_hash_contract.rule: the fresh independent verifier records these two file hashes at the accepted candidate; a metadata-only acceptance patch must preserve them exactly, and must preserve the semantic fingerprint for spec and fixture
exact_artifact_hash_contract.note: this defines the comparison contract only, not any future hash value

owner_clarification_records.title: NARROW_NORMATIVE_RUNTIME-BOUNDARY CLARIFICATION
owner_clarification_records.scope: Clarifies runtime representation domain, authority separation, representation-guard permission, expected-vs-internal-fault distinction, and diagnostic scope. Not final Slice 1A acceptance.

audit_metadata.immutability_note: Verifier findings and original severities are immutable audit history; the owner disposition here does not rewrite, erase, or reduce the recorded verifier finding.
detailed_boundary_reopen_triggers: current implementation profile expands beyond built-in JSON values
adapter or normalization changes admitting non-JSON runtime objects
guard expands into field-level, grammar, or event-type checks
exact diagnostic code/path/keyword/message/order becomes externally normative
direct admission of models, proxies, dataclasses, or custom mappings

od_s1a_008_decision_record.title: Stable Requirement Declaration Mapping
recommended_draft_unchanged: do not infer ids through hashing or normalization
bind it to the exact TaskContract digest

owner_must_decide.question: whether unknown or deprecated values BLOCK, REVIEW_REQUIRED or require explicit migration

od_s1a_008_decision_record.note: This record is COMPLETE as a pending decision: it enumerates every owner question and every pre-A3 declaration field. It does NOT choose any answer; A3 (Slice 1C-1) remains blocked until owner acceptance. No mapping is implemented.

deterministic_identity.set_normalization_rule: each set is lexicographically sorted and deduplicated

canonicalization_contract.object_keys: lexicographically sorted by the serializer; JSON insertion order is NOT semantic
canonicalization_contract.digest: 'sha256:' + 64 lowercase hex over the canonical JSON bytes

validation_levels.STRUCTURALLY_VALIDATED: exact typed field set + typed values + canonical collection form + no runtime/self-referential fields, and, when the canonical input is supplied, equality of every binding already present in both artifacts (task identity, all eight source bindings, policy/evaluator versions, evaluation mode, record-schema version/digest, canonicalization version/digest) plus record.input_digest == canonical_digest(canonical input); binding equality proves the record is bound to that input, never that the disposition or reasons are the production evaluator's semantic result for it
validation_levels.IDENTITY_RECOMPUTED: decision_digest recomputes from the record's own deterministic decision fields and policy_record_digest recomputes from the canonical payload; identity consistency only, never a semantic recomputation from facts
validation_levels.SEMANTIC_REPLAY_NOT_PERFORMED: always, for every Slice 1A validation result: no Slice 1A artifact, helper, manifest control, or test replays a decision through a production evaluator, so none may prove or claim semantically correct disposition, reason selection, or precedence application; a semantically forged but internally consistent policy result can be STRUCTURALLY_VALIDATED and IDENTITY_RECOMPUTED and must never be reported as semantically replay verified
validation_levels.SEMANTICALLY_REPLAY_VERIFIED: SLICE_1B_PROTOCOL_ONLY: requires re-executing the production pure evaluator over the canonical input and obtaining a byte-identical decision and record; no Slice 1A artifact or helper may return this status for a policy result

deterministic_identity.structural_validation_totality: every artifact structural validator is total over malformed JSON-compatible values: it returns structured validation failures, never raises, and fails closed on invalid types, booleans in typed positions, malformed collections, malformed digests, unknown fields, runtime fields, self-digest fields, unsorted or duplicated canonical collections, invalid partitions, and source/schema/canonicalization binding disagreement

event_identity_equality_matrix.applies_to: every event whose subject_ref.kind is git_candidate with a non-null digest

causal_semantics.root_creation_rule: each CHANGEGATE_EVALUATION_COMPLETED establishes an independent root keyed by its evaluation event id
causal_semantics.changed_candidate_new_root_rule: a changed candidate requires a new evaluation root; it can never continue an existing chain

semantic_controls.subject: policy_record
semantic_controls.predicate: record field SET == typed contract field set (+ policy_record_digest); no unknown, missing, or duplicate-under-another-key field; JSON key insertion order is NOT semantic

semantic_controls.subject: policy_record
semantic_controls.predicate: every field satisfies its typed_field_contract descriptor (type, grammar, enum membership, taxonomy/universe membership)

semantic_controls.subject: policy_record
semantic_controls.predicate: every digest field matches sha256:<64 lowercase hex> (or the exact sentinel for approval_digest_or_sentinel)

semantic_controls.subject: policy_record
semantic_controls.predicate: set-like lists are sorted and deduplicated; blocking ∪ review == complete with blocking ∩ review == ∅; required == satisfied ∪ invalid ∪ missing pairwise disjoint; validators reject non-normalized issued records and never silently normalize

semantic_controls.subject: policy_record
semantic_controls.predicate: record.decision_digest == canonical_digest(normative decision payload derived from the record's own fields); an arbitrary rehashed payload can never satisfy this

semantic_controls.subject: policy_record
semantic_controls.predicate: policy_record_digest == canonical_digest(payload)

semantic_controls.subject: policy_record
semantic_controls.predicate: no runtime/application field and no self-digest inside the payload

semantic_controls.subject: policy_record
semantic_controls.predicate: the same valid object with different JSON key insertion order is accepted and has the same canonical identity

semantic_controls.subject: authority_boundary
semantic_controls.predicate: the pure evaluator is the sole semantic producer of the decision and record payload; the same complete replay key yields exactly one decision and one record; no mechanism can produce a second policy record for the same replay key

semantic_controls.subject: authority_boundary
semantic_controls.predicate: Slice 1A contains no replacement, override, exception, expiry, authorization, or authority-binding semantics (OD-S1A-009); all such semantics remain pending under OD-S1A-005

semantic_controls.subject: authority_boundary
semantic_controls.predicate: only the designated evaluation-record writer persists the payload and persistence never alters a semantic field

semantic_controls.subject: evaluation_identity
semantic_controls.predicate: every evaluation_id is globally unique and maps to one lineage

semantic_controls.subject: evaluation_identity
semantic_controls.predicate: a downstream event preserves its root's evaluation_id

semantic_controls.subject: lineage
semantic_controls.predicate: the policy lineage is the six-field tuple including evaluation_id

semantic_controls.subject: lineage
semantic_controls.predicate: a root's lineage never changes; there is no replacement lineage and no lineage switch

semantic_controls.subject: lineage
semantic_controls.predicate: every explicit predecessor of an event shares one identical lineage; disagreement is ambiguous and rejected

semantic_controls.subject: lineage
semantic_controls.predicate: multiple independent evaluation roots and interleaved streams are supported

semantic_controls.subject: lineage
semantic_controls.predicate: a causal reference cycle is rejected

semantic_controls.subject: lineage
semantic_controls.predicate: a changed candidate requires a new evaluation root

semantic_controls.subject: policy_record
semantic_controls.predicate: Slice 1A structural validation returns at most STRUCTURALLY_VALIDATED, IDENTITY_RECOMPUTED, and SEMANTIC_REPLAY_NOT_PERFORMED; input-digest and binding equality are structural binding checks, never semantic replay; SEMANTICALLY_REPLAY_VERIFIED is a Slice 1B protocol status that no Slice 1A artifact may return for a policy result

semantic_controls.subject: deterministic_identity
semantic_controls.predicate: policy_record_schema_digest == canonical_digest(deterministic_identity.typed_field_contract); the published digest is executably derived from the exact typed contract object the validators and tests use, never hand-maintained

semantic_controls.subject: policy_record
semantic_controls.predicate: every structural validator is total over malformed JSON-compatible values and fails closed with structured errors; a validator crash on user-controlled input is a contract violation

semantic_controls.subject: policy_record
semantic_controls.predicate: when both the canonical input and the record are presented, every binding already present in both is equal: task identity, all eight source bindings, policy/evaluator versions, evaluation mode, record-schema version/digest, canonicalization version/digest, and record.input_digest == canonical_digest(canonical input); no new binding is added

semantic_controls.subject: authority_boundary
semantic_controls.predicate: no active Slice 1A machine semantic (reason taxonomy, golden case, manifest object, validator, or fingerprint input) carries an override or exception classification, including any renamed equivalent describing exception eligibility, human resolvability, override permission, or post-verdict authority action; OD-S1A-005 remains pending

semantic_controls.subject: event_identity
semantic_controls.predicate: for every event, event.task_ref == event.decision_ref.task_ref

semantic_controls.subject: event_identity
semantic_controls.predicate: for every event whose subject_ref.kind is git_candidate with a non-null digest, event.subject_ref.digest == event.decision_ref.candidate_digest

semantic_controls.subject: event_identity
semantic_controls.predicate: for every CHANGEGATE_EVALUATION_COMPLETED event, event.context_digest == event.decision_ref.input_digest

causal_semantics.immutable_lineage_rule: a root's six-field policy lineage is immutable; no event may mutate, replace, or switch it

authority_boundary.persistence_writer: designated ChangeGate evaluation-record writer; must not alter, replace, supplement, or reinterpret any semantic field
authority_boundary.audit_journal: append-only; non-authoritative; references immutable policy results
authority_boundary.human_actors: cannot produce or persist a policy decision payload
authority_boundary.review_event_in_slice_1a: DELETED (Path B) — review/approval events belong to the future governance slice
authority_boundary.od_s1a_005_dependency: PENDING_OWNER_DECISION — gates the future governance slice; does not block Slice 1A or Slice 1B
authority_boundary.active_override_exception_classification: ABSENT — no reason-taxonomy entry, golden case, or manifest object carries an override or exception classification field; the removed machine values (overrideability, exception-required, human-resolvability classes) may not be reintroduced under any name while OD-S1A-005 is pending; reintroduction is a semantic change requiring fresh verification

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: exactly 20 closed grammatical codes with unique ranks and a closed entry field set
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: an entry with an extra classification field, bad grammar, or duplicate rank fails
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: adding any classification field to a taxonomy entry fails the closed-shape check and changes the fingerprint

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: an independently pinned code-to-disposition table matches the fixture and manifest; all three dispositions and both authorities appear across golden cases
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: a disposition drift between the pinned table and fixture fails
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: changing any default disposition diverges from the pinned table

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: an independently pinned rank table matches fixture and manifest; every case's expected primary reason equals the pinned minimum-rank derivation
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: coordinated rank drift in fixture and manifest alone fails against the pinned table
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: any precedence change alters the semantic fingerprint

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: a pinned test-only oracle maps every declared fact state and reproduces all 41 golden expectations from facts alone; the verifier rule is total over 12 combinations
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: an unmapped fact state or an expectation the oracle cannot reproduce fails
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: single-fact deviations change the oracle-derived outcome

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: required == satisfied ∪ invalid ∪ missing, pairwise disjoint, for every case; empty-bundle cases pinned in both directions
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: an overlapping or non-exhaustive partition in a record fails validation
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: moving a requirement id between partitions without updating the union fails

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: requirement and evidence universes are disjoint per case and per record
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: a requirement id inside an evidence set (and vice versa) fails validation
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: namespace swap after full rederivation still fails

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: production canonical_digest output is accepted by every digest field in record and schema
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: bare hex, uppercase, wrong algorithm, wrong length, and whitespace forms are rejected by both validators
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: replacing any digest with a malformed form fails after rehash

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: no raw-content field exists in the schema and no credential shape exists in the fixture
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: content-bearing reference values are rejected by the schema grammar
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: a credential-shaped or content-bearing reference value fails schema validation

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: the complete replay key is exact and every key component change changes input identity
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: a mutated binding that leaves input_digest unchanged would fail
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: all 16 binding/fact mutations change the canonical input digest

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: the published policy_record_schema_digest equals the independent canonical digest of the typed field contract
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: a stale or hand-written digest value fails the derivation equality
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: field rename, type change, and collection-semantics change each change the derived digest; metadata outside the typed contract does not

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: canonicalization_contract_digest equals the canonical digest of the canonicalization contract and is bound into the canonical input
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: a canonicalization version or digest drift between input and manifest fails binding consistency
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: canonicalization binding changes change input identity and the fingerprint

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: every binding present in both record and canonical input is equal
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: drift in any of the eight source bindings, versions, mode, or schema binding is rejected even after full rederivation
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: each binding drifted independently fails with a structured mismatch error

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: decision_digest recomputes from the record's own deterministic decision fields
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: every decision-field mutation followed only by outer rehash fails DECISION_DIGEST_NOT_DERIVED
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: ten semantic decision mutations are each rejected

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: policy_record_digest recomputes from the canonical payload; key order is not identity
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: an outer mutation without rehash fails RECORD_DIGEST_MISMATCH
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: arbitrary record digests and stale payloads are rejected

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: the structural validator accepts any JSON-compatible value and returns structured errors
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: malformed scalars, collections, digests, unknown/runtime fields never crash and never classify
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: every malformed-value class yields errors and empty classifications

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: valid results carry exactly STRUCTURALLY_VALIDATED, IDENTITY_RECOMPUTED, SEMANTIC_REPLAY_NOT_PERFORMED
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: a semantically forged but internally consistent record is structurally valid yet never reported as semantically replay verified
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: claiming SEMANTICALLY_REPLAY_VERIFIED in Slice 1A changes the fingerprint and fails the boundary test

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: event.task_ref equals decision_ref.task_ref for every event
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: an envelope task mismatch is rejected as TASK_IDENTITY_MISMATCH
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: envelope task mutated independently of the decision reference is rejected

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: subject_ref.digest equals decision_ref.candidate_digest for candidate subjects
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: a subject candidate mismatch is rejected as CANDIDATE_IDENTITY_MISMATCH
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: subject digest mutated independently of the decision reference is rejected

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: context_digest equals decision_ref.input_digest for evaluation events
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: a context-digest mismatch is rejected as INPUT_DIGEST_REFERENCE_MISMATCH
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: context digest mutated independently of the decision reference is rejected

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: each root registers a unique evaluation id and downstream events preserve it
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: duplicate root evaluation ids and downstream evaluation-id drift are rejected
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: duplicated and drifted evaluation ids each produce their exact failure code

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: a root's six-field lineage is immutable and repeated exactly by every descendant
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: lineage divergence on any of the six fields is rejected
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: a changed candidate continuing an existing chain is rejected; a new root is required

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: an event with multiple explicit predecessors validates every predecessor and requires one shared lineage
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: conflicting predecessor lineages are rejected as AMBIGUOUS_PREDECESSOR_LINEAGE
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: a rollback citing a merge from one root and a validation from another is rejected

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: acyclic chains validate
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: a causal reference cycle is rejected as CAUSAL_CYCLE
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: a two-event reference cycle is rejected

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: OD-S1A-009 is ACCEPTED, OD-S1A-001…008 remain pending, and every pending decision has at least one marked golden case
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: a silently resolved owner decision fails the status check
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: resolving OD-S1A-005 in the manifest boundary changes the fingerprint

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: the exact-artifact hash contract names both executable artifacts without hard-coding hashes
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: a governance contract missing either compared file fails
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: removing the comparison contract changes the fingerprint

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: metadata-only acceptance changes preserve the semantic fingerprint
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: a semantic change necessarily alters the fingerprint
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: eighteen required semantic mutations each change the fingerprint while metadata does not

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: every event type validates with its full local contract
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: a minimal common-envelope-only instance is invalid for every event type
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: removing any per-event required field fails schema validation

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: the manifest binds exactly the accepted Slice 1A control set and the fingerprint hashes it
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: a missing required control or an unexpected control fails the closed-set check
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: mutation: removing or altering any named control changes the fingerprint

contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: no replacement record path exists in any artifact
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: no helper can fabricate a second policy record for the same input
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: reintroduction attack: a replacement object or helper is rejected by the schema and the closed manifest control set

contract_to_test_coverage.normative_source: R5 record registry (deleted by Model B)
contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: no registry helper or fixture path exists
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: no lookup can serve a replacement record
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: reintroduction attack: registry helper names are absent from the artifact test module

contract_to_test_coverage.normative_source: R5 schema replacement_decision_ref (deleted by Model B)
contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: the schema has no replacement reference object
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: an event carrying replacement_decision_ref fails schema validation
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: reintroduction attack: adding replacement_decision_ref to a valid event is rejected by additionalProperties

contract_to_test_coverage.normative_source: R5 CHANGEGATE_REVIEW_OVERRIDDEN (deleted by Model B, Path B)
contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: the closed six-type event vocabulary contains no review event
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: a review/override event type fails the enum
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: reintroduction attack: CHANGEGATE_REVIEW_OVERRIDDEN and CHANGEGATE_REVIEW_RECORDED instances are schema-rejected

contract_to_test_coverage.normative_source: R5 override lineage switch (deleted by Model B)
contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: policy lineage is immutable per root; no switch branch exists
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: any lineage divergence from the referenced root is rejected
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: reintroduction attack: an event switching to a second decision identity is rejected as lineage divergence

contract_to_test_coverage.normative_source: R6 classification-field residue (removed by R7 under OD-S1A-009)
contract_to_test_coverage.positive_oracle: no artifact carries the removed classification field or its removed machine values
contract_to_test_coverage.negative_oracle: closed entry/case field sets reject any classification field under any name
contract_to_test_coverage.mutation_or_reintroduction_oracle: reintroduction attack: reintroducing the field or values fails the closed-shape checks and changes the fingerprint

representation.json_number: finite int or float, excluding bool

not_admitted_directly: collections.abc.Mapping implementations that are not builtin dict

runtime_authority_boundary.audit_history_preservation_rule: Verifier findings and original severities are immutable audit history. Owner disposition must be recorded separately. Acceptance must not rewrite, erase, or retroactively reduce verifier history.
runtime_authority_boundary.boundary_re_review_rule: The representation boundary must be reviewed when an assumption required for representation-guard and schema equivalence ceases to hold.

od_s1a_009_decision_record.title: Slice 1A Deterministic Evaluation Boundary
decision: ChangeGate Slice 1A defines deterministic policy-evaluation contracts only.
The pure evaluator is the sole semantic producer of MergeEligibilityDecision and the deterministic PolicyEvaluationRecord payload.

Only the designated ChangeGate evaluation-record writer may persist the evaluator-produced payload; persistence must not alter any semantic field, must not author a second decision, and must not convert human review metadata into a policy result.

Same canonical MergeEligibilityPolicyInput + policy version + evaluator version + evaluation mode + policy-record schema version + canonicalization version and contract → same semantic decision, same canonical PolicyEvaluationRecord, same decision digest, same policy-record digest.
A policy decision and its policy record are immutable.
must_not_define_or_permit: mutation or replacement of a policy verdict
a policy-record registry whose purpose is replacement or override
od_s1a_009_decision_record.review_recording_rule: Human review activity may be recorded only as non-authoritative audit metadata referencing the immutable policy result; a review record must not change disposition, change decision identity, authorize an action, establish an exception, or switch policy lineage.
od_s1a_009_decision_record.future_direction: Approval, exception and bounded action-authorization semantics remain pending under OD-S1A-005 and belong to a separate future governance slice, using a separate authority artifact referencing the immutable policy verdict. OD-S1A-009 does not decide that artifact's name, schema, issuer, scope, expiry, revocation, supersession, consumption, or replay-protection rules.

slice_impact.slice_1b: typed facts and pure deterministic evaluator; not blocked by OD-S1A-005
slice_impact.slice_1c: evidence-to-facts adapter and application vertical; blocked by OD-S1A-008
slice_impact.governance_slice: approval, exception and bounded action authorization; blocked by OD-S1A-005

od_s1a_009_decision_record.non_decisions: OD-S1A-001 through OD-S1A-008 otherwise remain pending.
